issues: 1x silhouette thin and small — body reads as a vertical sliver, flame barely distinguishable from handle
charm is functional-classic rather than sticker-cute — no soft roundness or personality pop
light-bg contrast acceptable but handle mid-tones blend slightly into cream
reasoning: At 4x the torch sprite is clean and well-crafted pixel art: warm amber flame with a pleasing organic shape, decorative bracket base, good contrast on both dark and light backgrounds. The orange-on-dark reads beautifully — flame pops with strong contrast. On the light/cream bg it holds up, though the brown handle loses some edge crispness. Silhouette at 4x is instantly readable as 'torch' — vertical body, flame crown, decorative foot — classic fantasy readability. However at 1x the sprite shrinks to a very thin vertical form where the flame and body merge into an undifferentiated sliver; the silhouette survives only because the orange blob on top implies flame. Charm score is moderate: the sprite is competent and warm-toned but lacks the rounded, toy-like softness the rubric demands for a clear pass. It reads more as 'utility prop' than 'charming sticker.' Edge cleanliness is good — no stray pixels, crisp outlines. Overall: passes readability and contrast tests, falls short on the cuteness/personality dimension required for a full pass.
